content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers - A1,A2,‚ãØ‚Äâ,ANA_1, A_2, \cdots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚ãØ,AN‚Äã. For each index iii find the maximum number of inversions in the entire array you can get by changing this element to any real\textbf{real}real number.

Note: The number of inversions in an array AAA is the number of pairs of indices iii, jjj such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i < j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N and Ai>AjA_i > A_jAi‚Äã>Aj‚Äã


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of 2 lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN  ‚Äî the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers denoting the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, NNN space separated integers where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the answer for the ithi^{th}ith index of the array.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.
The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1042 \cdot 10^42‚ãÖ104
There are at most 50 tests with N>10N>10N>10 and there is at most 1 test with N>50N>50N>50.
The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1032 \cdot 10^32‚ãÖ103.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: The initial array is [1,2][1, 2][1,2].

Let us now try to modify A1A_1A1‚Äã to get the maximum number of inversions. We can set A1=3A_1 = 3A1‚Äã=3, and the new array [3,2][3, 2][3,2] has 1 inversion. 
We can show that this is the maximum number of inversions we can achieve by modifying A1A_1A1‚Äã, and so the first output is 111.
Let us now try to modify A2A_2A2‚Äã to get the maximum number of inversions. We can set A2=0.5A_2 = 0.5A2‚Äã=0.5, and the new array [1,0.5][1, 0.5][1,0.5] has 1 inversion. 
We can show that this is the maximum number of inversions we can achieve by modifying A2A_2A2‚Äã, and so the second output is 111.
